# Overview

|                 |                          |
| --------------- | ------------------------ |
| Game title      | Shape fight              |
| Genere          | Rougelike, combat, cards |
| Platfrom        | Desktop (windows/Linux)  |
| Target audience | Adults, casual           |
| Player count    | Singleplayer             |
| Playtime        | Infinate                 |
| Art style       | cartoon                  |

## High concept
A 3D top down shooter game where the goal is to survive for as long as possible, the player is given cards to pick from which can change how the player progresses through the game they can do anything from changing the player walkspeed to making the player shoot multiple times.
## USP
This game brings 2 very popular categories of games together: card games and top down games. It creates an enjoyable style of game that keeps the player interested for a long time and adds enough variety to not get boring over a long time. 

The game takes a different art style by making assets with simple cel shaded shapes this makes the game feel visually appealing as bright saturated colors stimulate the player which is supported by cel shading adding depth to the game.

The game will contain only a few mechanics however their simplicity makes the game very enjoyable as they go on infinitely the player will try to live as long as they can.
## Inspiration
inspiration by a few other games that were found online which include:
- ### Rounds
	- In this game you fight another player in order to win after every round you are given several cards to pick from, each of these cards give random effects to the player allowing them to become more powerful in order to defeat the other player, The cards mechanic inspired the creation of this game
- ### Project zomboid
	- This game is a top down survival game, in order to survive the player must scavenge houses and cities for resources such as food, guns, and weapons, they must defend against a zombie apocalypse. The top down perspective at an angle also inspired this game alongside using guns to attack enemies 
Compared to these games this game will be quite different. The main goal of this game is to create a top down PVE shooter that takes advantage of card mechanics and RNG. This takes the PVE idea of project zomboid and the random cards from Rounds to make a unique concept.
# Gameplay
## Core gameplay loop
The game repeats indefinitely as the player keeps playing till they die, the game starts by attacking enemies after gaining enough score the player will be given a few cards to pick from this is where the game loops and the player is given a small boost while continue to play through the game.
## Core Mechanics:
- ### Health
	- Both the player and the enemies will have health the goal is to kill enemies by attacking them until their health is 0
	- The player also has health however it is significantly more than the enemies at 100 health by default similarly to enemies if the players health goes below 0 they will die and loose the game
- ### Combat
	- The player can shoot at the enemies to damage and kill them the way the player shoots and other aspects can be modified by the cards, the enemies attack by physically attacking the player
- ### Special ability
	- The player will also be given a special ability which can give a special buff to the player or attack enemies in a specific way, they will only have one special that they can use at a time which creates a strategy as they player gets a special ability they can take advantage of
- ### Enemies
	- Enemies will spawn sporadically around the world they attempt to attack the player by moving towards them, the spawn rate, walkspeed, and other factors can also be affected by cards
- ### Scaling difficulty
	- As time passes while the player plays the game the difficulty of the game will slowly become increasingly difficult things like enemy health or spawn rate will be increased over time this allows the difficulty to be balanced as the player also progresses overtime and becomes more powerful the enemies will also stay on the players level
- ### Cards
	- the card system is a major mechanic the after the player kills enough enemies and gains hits a threshold the game pauses and lets the player select from a few cards that can change any parameter in the game after they select from a card the card changed the game depending on what it said this can do anything increased or decreasing difficulty or how the game works
- ### Top down perspective / movement
	- The player is displayed from a top down/birdseye perspective meaning that they get an overview of the world as well as the focus on the player. The player's movement is also slightly different to normal; the player can still be controlled using WASD however they can aim with and shoot by holding the left mouse button down; they can also dash which gives them a velocity boost by pressing shift
- ### Shaders
	- cel shading is also another mechanic that will be used to make the game more visually appealing towards players and stimulating, outlines will also be used to add depth into the game and make it more visually appealing to the player
	- Outline shaders will also be added by adding an outline on every object it adds depth to the game making the game more appealing towards players

## Player win & lose conditions
- ### loose
	- the player can lose the game after they are killed by the enemies or if they give up
- ### Win
	- There is no win condition as the game goes on indefinitely however the player will be given a high score after dying this score can be saved to a save file inside the system folder appdata
## Player goal
The player's goal is to survive as long as possible without dying, their goal is to become more and more powerful with zero limits to how powerful they can become
## Player progression
The player will start the game with a slow firing weapon as they progress they have to kill enemies and collect points that the drop eventually after collecting enough points the player will be given cards that can make them more powerful in several ways by modifying parameters they can change the following:
- Health 
- Walkspeed
- Dash speed
- Dash count
- Attack damaged
- Attack speed
- Attack amount
- Special ability
- Bullet velocity
- Enemy health
- Enemy attack
- Likely to add more over time
## Controls

| Function | Key    |
| -------- | ------ |
| Move     | WASD   |
| Dash     | SHIFT  |
| Special  | RMB    |
| Pause    | ESCAPE |
## world
### Setting:
The game is mainly focused on gameplay over looks however the world will be set inside a flat grass lands area with a few props like trees and rocks.

#### Graybox:
Below is a basic graybox using blender. You can see a preview of a segment of the world as mentioned above. The design of the world takes a one a simple approach, the main landscape will be a simple flat plane with several props scattered on top of it 

Enemies will spawn sporadically around the world and factors like spawn rate can be changed overtime or by cards
![](data:image/png;base64,)
### Art: 
My game will have a specific art style to it, I want to create a cartoon style game using cel shading and simple shapes as characters, as well as cel shading. I also want to add outlines around objects to make them stand out more and more visually appealing towards players.

Below are images that helped inspire my games art style

![[Pasted image 20260326131715.png]]
![[Pasted image 20260326131739.png]]

## Assets:
This game will use several assets that I will be making the majority of 3D models will be done using blender as well as texturing will also be done in blender and adobe substance
- 3D models
	- Characters
		- Player - cube
		- Enemies - sphere
	- Items
		- Special item
		- +1 score item
	- Props / foliage
		- Trees
		- Bushes
		- Rocks
- Textures
	- Textures for assets will be very simple taking a solid color or a few colors to add depth
	- Large texture like grass will have multiple colors with noise to create dark areas and variety
- UI
	- Symbols / images for cards
	- Fonts
	- UI elements and symbols

## UI:
The game will have several UI elements to support each mechanic in the game. 
A list of each UI element in my game:
- HUD overlay - score, health etc
	- The HUD will contain several elements this allows the player to see their stats like their score or high score, another part will show what cards that the player has obtained the HUD will show a small preview of each card that the player has however it will limit the amount of cards on the screen and show the seven most recently obtained cards this will stop the screen from showing too many cards and blocking the viewport
- Pause menu - pause the game with options allowing you to quit, quit to desktop, etc
	- The pause menu will contain multiple menus inside it, it will contain several options allowing the player to resume the game, quit to menu or desktop, it will also add an options menu allowing the player to change options like their graphics settings.
- Options menu - contains several options allowing the player to change settings like graphics
	- This menu will contain options allowing the player to change things like graphics settings allowing the player to change the visual quality of the game which allows the game to be played on lower end / cheaper hardware.
	- Difficulty settings will also be under the options menu allowing the player to change the difficulty of the game making it more difficult or easier to play the game.
- Cards - main mechanic allows you to pick from 3 cards that can change gameplay
	- The cards UI will display 3 cards the cards will have a symbol on the top and details of what the card does on the bottom half of each card
	- Using a data table in unreal engine which will store all the information about the cards including:
		- Images / symbol
		- Card colors
		- Description
		- Name
		- Positive effects
		- Negative effects
		- Special effects
	- When a card is selected the effects listed below will change parameters in the game changing the difficulty and how the player attacks the enemies.
## UI design:
The design of my UI will attempt to match the style of my game i will be using smooth fonts with outlining to match the style of the game

The design of each aspect of the user interface of the game matching the above section detailing what each element’s function is:

- HUD overlay ![](data:image/png;base64,)
    
	- The HUD will be persistent and will always show on the screen above is a sketch of the HUD in photoshop 
	- The layout of the HUD will have the score and high score on the top left, recently obtained cards on the top right, and health on the bottom left.
- Pause menu
	- The pause menu will pause the game hence the name, but it will also have several options with the layout having options on the left in a vertical list, the options will be: resume, options, stats, quit, quit to desktop
- Options menu
	- The options menu will contain several options allowing the player to change the graphics settings of the game, and other settings.
- Cards
	- The cards will be animated as they appear onto the screen they will drop down from the top on the screen with a staggered animation, they will also bounce to make the animation feel more fluent.
	- The design of the cards will match the style of the HUD to keep consistency.

## What to lean
To make this game I will have to learn several skills as well as use skills that I already have learnt. For all the skills that are required to make this game I have at least looked at how to do it and have a small understanding of them, the list of skills and details of them include:

- ### Blender
	- To 3D model everything in my game I will be using blender, I have a decent amount of experience with this software and understand the basics including: modeling, uv mapping, shading, and some animation.
- ### Unreal engine 5
	- To make the game I have chosen to use Unreal Engine 5, I have a lot of experience using this engine as well as I enjoy using this software the most out of any other 3D engine.
- ### UE5 AI
	- My game will have a super basic AI making the enemies move towards the player when in a range, however the engine comes with a built in template making super easy to set up a basic AI system
- ### UE5 UI
	- My game will be using a lot of UI aspects and will heavily rely of it to work, my game will have several user interfaces including: the HUD, pause menu, and cards menu
	- However, prior to this project I have learnt a lot about making UIs in UE5 and it is something that I enjoy so I feel that I am more than capable of making several UIs for my game
- ### Database systems (UE5 data tables)
	- To make my cards system I will need to make a database to store all of the different cards in my game, I will need to pull from a database and pick a random row to become a card to display and effect the game
	- This is a new thing to me as I have never worked with Database systems I will be using the built in data system inside unreal instead of something like SQL as it wont be a very complex database
- ### Shaders
	- Shaders will also be another skill that I will need to learn more specifically in UE5 I have found several tutorials online to make some shaders to make my game’s visuals significantly better
	- Making shaders will be something that I may or may not do as making shaders is quite difficult. If I do not get the time to then I will not be adding shaders to my game.
